_note: Corrected 2026-05-29 from the prior P3A_BLOCKED_MISSING_MAINNET_RPC record. The P3A worker was deployed OOB from the operator's host shell at 2026-05-29T07:56:44Z (confirmed via ~/Library/Preferences/.wrangler/logs + a live HTTP 200 probe). Earlier 'fetch failed' lines were intermittent connectivity / post-deploy metrics-dispatcher noise, not a failed deploy.
phase: Phase P3A-Run Mainnet Readonly Live-UI Deployment Test
deploySource: operator host shell (OOB), wrangler 4.94.0, deploy --config wrangler.workers.p3a.jsonc
transferStructuralReason: src/config/life-plus.ts isLive === (gateMode === 'live'); live-readonly !== 'live', so TRANSFER_ENABLED is always false regardless of any flag.
rpcExposureNote: NEXT_PUBLIC_SOLANA_RPC_URL is the public placeholder https://api.mainnet-beta.solana.com (no paid key). Server route /api/solana/lifepp-balance reads SOLANA_RPC_URL (server secret) only; with the secret unset it returns fail-soft rpc_not_configured. No RPC URL is returned in any API response.

connectivity.evidence: Host wrangler attempts 07:44-07:53 failed with 'fetch failed'; 07:56 deploy succeeded (CF API 200s). dash.cloudflare.com returned cf-mitigated: challenge (browser/IP challenge; does not block OAuth/API-token CLI once network is stable).
connectivity.remediation: Retry whoami/secret-put during a stable window; optionally use CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN + CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID (set locally, never committed) to avoid the OAuth refresh round-trip. OAuth is functional (deploy succeeded).

nextOperatorAction: npx wrangler whoami  (confirm token valid; retry on transient fetch failed), then  npx wrangler secret put SOLANA_RPC_URL --config wrangler.workers.p3a.jsonc  (during a stable Cloudflare API window), then run readonly smoke tests. No redeploy required for the secret to bind.
rollbackInstructions: Non-destructive. Rotate/remove the secret: npx wrangler secret put|delete SOLANA_RPC_URL --config wrangler.workers.p3a.jsonc (route reverts to fail-soft readonly). Or tear down the separate worker: npx wrangler delete --name ahin-io-p3a. Transfer/burn/signing/canary/public-payment flags stay false; root ahin.io and the ahin-io worker are never touched; default wrangler.workers.jsonc (devnet/mock) unaffected.
nextPhase: P3B (single foundation-wallet micro-transfer) remains BLOCKED — additionally requires allowlist + per-payment cap + kill-switch drill + OOB mint/treasury/ATA verification + explicit operator authorization.
